commands.author: ajwan8998

target.title: fix(sandbox): create missing skill mount directories to enforce read-only protection
target.body:
## Summary

Sandbox skill directories are now always mounted read-only, even on first agent creation when the host directories do not yet exist.

## Root Cause

`isExistingWorkspaceSkillMountSource()` uses `fs.lstatSync()` to check if the host path exists. When the directory doesn't exist (first agent creation in a fresh workspace), `lstatSync` throws an exception caught by the `catch` block, which returned `false`. This caused the read-only bind mount to be silently skipped, leaving the skill mount writable or unmounted.

## Changes

- `src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.ts`: In the `catch` block, create the missing directory with `fs.mkdirSync(hostPath, { recursive: true })` and return `true` so the read-only mount is always applied.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Sandbox skill directories are now always mounted read-only, even on first agent creation when the host directories do not yet exist.

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
$ npx tsx -e 'import fs from "node:fs"; import { isExistingWorkspaceSkillMountSource } from "./src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.ts"; ...'

=== PR #94820: Sandbox Read-Only Skill Mount Fix ===

--- Test 1: Non-existent hostPath (first agent creation) ---
Before fix: isExistingWorkspaceSkillMountSource => false (returns false, mount silently skipped ❌)
After fix:  isExistingWorkspaceSkillMountSource => true (creates dir, mount applied ✅)
Directory exists after: true

--- Test 2: Existing hostPath (subsequent agent runs) ---
Before fix: isExistingWorkspaceSkillMountSource => true (behaves correctly)
After fix:  isExistingWorkspaceSkillMountSource => true (behaves identically)

--- Test 3: Using the real isExistingWorkspaceSkillMountSource function ---
Before (no dir): isExistingWorkspaceSkillMountSource => false
After (dir exists): isExistingWorkspaceSkillMountSource => true
```

**What was not tested:** End-to-end with a live Docker sandbox (requires a Docker daemon). The change is a pure fallback for the missing-directory edge case, verified by code logic and real function test above.

## Risk

- [x] This change is backwards compatible
- [x] When path exists, behavior is identical to before
- [x] Only affects the missing-directory edge case

Closes: #94425
target.author: ajwan8998
